evals.prompt: Show me the RGOS dashboard. I want to see which clients are at risk and if there are any renewals coming up in the next 30 days.
evals.expected_output: Calls rgos_dashboard first for overview. Then calls rgos_list_clients with status=at_risk filter. Results presented as clean table. Upcoming renewals highlighted. No data made up - only what the tools return.

assertions.name: calls-rgos-dashboard
assertions.description: Response invokes rgos_dashboard tool (or explains it would) rather than making up a dashboard

assertions.name: uses-correct-filter-for-at-risk
assertions.description: rgos_list_clients is called with status=at_risk filter, not just listing all clients

assertions.name: presents-data-cleanly
assertions.description: Results are formatted as a table or structured list, not a wall of text

assertions.name: does-not-fabricate-data
assertions.description: Response does not invent client names or renewal dates - it relies on tool output

evals.prompt: Log 1.5 hours to the Noto Group HubSpot Lead Scoring project for today. It was for scoring model design work. Make it billable.
evals.expected_output: Confirms the details with user before logging (confirm hours, project, date, description). Then calls rgos_log_time with project_name fuzzy-matched to Noto Group, hours=1.5, description about scoring model design, billable=true, date=today.

assertions.name: confirms-before-logging
assertions.description: Explicitly confirms the details with the user before calling rgos_log_time (per the skill's Important Rules)

assertions.name: uses-correct-tool
assertions.description: Calls rgos_log_time (not rgos_create_task or another tool)

assertions.name: hours-are-decimal
assertions.description: Hours represented as 1.5 not '1 hour 30 min' or '90 min'
